app.footer: This application is made to raise awareness about greenhouse gas emissions.
app.error: An error occurred during calculation. Please make sure the backend server is running.

header.title: 🌿 Carbon Footprint Awareness
header.subtitle: Calculate how much greenhouse gas you emit in your daily life, see the impact, and discover ways to reduce it.

carbonForm.title: 🌍 Carbon Footprint Calculator
carbonForm.foodDesc: Search and add the foods you consume below, then enter your weekly amount.
carbonForm.wasteDesc: Select weekly amounts and disposal methods for each waste type.

carbonResult.equivalent: This is equivalent to the carbon absorbed by {{trees}} trees in one year.

tips.title: 💚 Tips to Reduce Your Carbon Footprint

items.title: Ride a bike
items.desc: Prefer cycling over driving for short distances. 1 km cycling = 0 kg CO₂.

items.title: Public transport
items.desc: Using bus/train instead of private car reduces emissions by 70%.

items.title: Reduce air travel
items.desc: Travel by train when possible. Short flights have the highest emissions per km.

items.title: Save energy
items.desc: Use LED bulbs, unplug electronics. You can save 500 kg CO₂ per year.

items.title: Prevent food waste
items.desc: 1.3 billion tons of food is wasted yearly, responsible for 8% of global emissions. Buy what you need, use leftovers.

items.title: Recycle
items.desc: Sort your waste. 1 ton of recycling = 1 ton of CO₂ saved.

items.title: Plant trees
items.desc: A tree absorbs ~22 kg CO₂ per year. Every 10 trees = 1 flight.

items.title: Renewable energy
items.desc: Switch to a green energy tariff to make your electricity usage carbon neutral.

gallery.title: 🌍 Environmental Impact of Greenhouse Gases
gallery.subtitle: Climate change threatens not just temperatures but the entire ecosystem. The visuals below show the destruction caused by greenhouse gas emissions.

images.title: Polar Bear - Disappearing Ice
images.description: Global warming is melting ice caps, polar bears are losing their habitat. By 2040, there may be no summer ice left in the Arctic.

images.title: Melting Glacier - Iceland
images.description: Glaciers are melting at record speeds. Many could disappear entirely within 20 years. Sea levels are rising, coastal cities face flooding.

images.title: California Wildfire - Night View
images.description: Greenhouse gases intensify wildfires. In 2023, Canadian fires burned 18 million hectares. Millions of animals lost their habitats.

images.title: Burning Forest - Fire Disaster
images.description: Millions of hectares of forest burn every year. Carbon emissions make fires more frequent and destructive. Ecosystems suffer irreversible damage.

images.title: Orangutan - Rainforest Destruction
images.description: Rainforests destroyed for palm oil production have pushed orangutans to the brink of extinction. Every hour, an area the size of 300 football fields is lost.

images.title: Drought - Cracked Earth
images.description: Global warming intensifies drought every year. Millions face water scarcity. Farmlands turn to desert, food crisis grows.

images.title: Factory Pollution - Air Pollution
images.description: Fossil fuels are the main cause of greenhouse gas emissions. 8 million people die each year from pollution-related diseases.

images.title: Plastic Pollution - Beach Trash
images.description: 11 million tons of plastic enter the ocean yearly. By 2050, there will be more plastic than fish in the sea. Marine life dies from plastic waste.

images.title: Flood - Submerged Village
images.description: Floods have become more frequent and destructive due to climate change. Millions are forced to leave their homes. Sea levels could rise 1 meter by 2100.

images.title: Landfill - Methane Gas
images.description: Decomposing organic waste in landfills produces methane, 25 times more harmful than CO₂. Landfills account for 12% of global methane emissions.

images.title: Industrial Plant - Aerial View
images.description: Oil refineries and industrial facilities are among the largest sources of greenhouse gases. 36 billion tons of CO₂ are released into the atmosphere annually.

images.title: Ship Exhaust - Maritime Pollution
images.description: Maritime transport accounts for 3% of global CO₂ emissions. Ships also emit sulfur oxides that cause acid rain.

images.title: Deforestation - Cut Trees
images.description: 10 million hectares of forest are destroyed every year. Forests are carbon sinks; when destroyed, stored carbon is released, accelerating climate crisis.

images.title: Coral Reef - Warming Oceans
images.description: As ocean temperatures rise, corals bleach and die. 50% of the world's coral reefs have died in the last 30 years. Marine ecosystems are collapsing.

images.title: Smog and Air Pollution - Mexico City
images.description: Air pollution in cities both contributes to emissions and threatens the health of millions. 9 out of 10 people breathe polluted air.

campaignsPage.title: 🎯 Campaigns
campaignsPage.desc: Join campaigns organized to fight climate change and see your impact.
campaignsPage.compostDesc: Prevent methane gas formation by composting your kitchen waste.
campaignsPage.greenTariffTitle: Choose Green Tariff, Take a Stand
campaignsPage.greenTariffDesc: Choose the green tariff, take a stand — but will your wallet hurt?

compostingCampaign.title: Composting Campaign
compostingCampaign.tagline: Instead of throwing your kitchen waste away, prevent <1>methane gas</1> by composting. Methane is <3>25 times more harmful</3> than CO₂.
compostingCampaign.statGlobalShare: Share of global emissions from food waste
compostingCampaign.statSaving: CO₂ prevented per 1 kg organic waste composted (weekly)
compostingCampaign.noParticipants: No one has joined yet. Be the first!
compostingCampaign.joinedMessage: ✅ You've successfully joined! You're among {{count}} people.
compostingCampaign.howToStep1Desc: Set up a compost bin or pile on your balcony or in your garden.
compostingCampaign.howToStep2Desc: Fruit/vegetable peels, eggshells, coffee grounds, dry leaves.
compostingCampaign.howToStep3Desc: Mix once a week. In 3-6 months, your nutrient-rich fertilizer will be ready!
compostingCampaign.howToStep4Desc: Use the resulting fertilizer in your garden or pots.
compostingCampaign.howToNote: ⚠️ Meat, dairy, and oily waste are not suitable for composting. Only use plant-based waste.

greenTariffCampaign.title: Choose Green Tariff, Take a Stand
greenTariffCampaign.tagline: A green tariff is a plan where you buy electricity generated from renewable sources (wind, solar, geothermal).
greenTariffCampaign.heardTitle: 🙋 First Time Hearing About Green Tariff
greenTariffCampaign.heardDesc: First time hearing about green tariff and want to say "I'm a green tariff user too"? Let's raise our voices to create awareness!
greenTariffCampaign.heardStat: people said they first heard about green tariff
greenTariffCampaign.heardButton: ⚡ I also first heard about green tariff
greenTariffCampaign.heardJoined: ✅ Thank you! You contributed to green tariff awareness. You're with {{count}} people now!
greenTariffCampaign.userTitle: ✅ I'm a Green Tariff User Too
greenTariffCampaign.userDesc: Have you switched to a green tariff? If you've switched with your electricity provider, show up here! Let's see how many people have chosen green tariff.
greenTariffCampaign.userButton: ✅ I'm a green tariff user too
greenTariffCampaign.userJoined: ✅ Great! Thank you for switching to green tariff. You're with {{count}} people now!
greenTariffCampaign.whyExpensive: 💸 Why is green tariff so expensive?
greenTariffCampaign.whyExpensiveDesc: "I choose nature" while punishing your wallet. Green tariff is much more expensive than standard tariff. <1>But why?</1>
greenTariffCampaign.statPriceDiff: Green tariff is this much more expensive than standard tariff
greenTariffCampaign.statStandardLow: Standard low tier unit price (≤8 kWh/day)
greenTariffCampaign.statStandardHigh: Standard high tier unit price (>8 kWh/day)
greenTariffCampaign.calculatorHint: Enter your monthly consumption to see the effect of the green tariff on your bill.
greenTariffCampaign.whatIsGreenDesc1: A green tariff is a plan that allows you to purchase electricity generated from renewable energy sources (wind, solar, geothermal, biomass). Part of your bill goes directly to renewable energy producers.
greenTariffCampaign.whatIsGreenDesc2: Sounds great, right? But here's the harsh truth: <1>green tariff is much more expensive than standard tariff</1>. In Turkey, when a residential subscriber chooses the green tariff, the active energy price is 345.47 kr/kWh. Yet the same subscriber pays only 49.41 kr/kWh for the low tier and 189.58 kr/kWh for the high tier on the standard tariff.
greenTariffCampaign.detailTitle: 📋 Tariff Comparison (Low Voltage Single Term Residential)
greenTariffCampaign.fairnessDesc: The green tariff price is applied at the ceiling price of 345.47 kr/kWh set by EPDK. This price is well above the cost of renewable energy in the free market. Yet solar and wind energy now produce electricity <1>cheaper than coal and natural gas</1>. So why is green tariff more expensive?
greenTariffCampaign.critique1: Double margin: Distribution companies charge the same distribution fee on both standard and green tariffs, then add a green premium on top.
greenTariffCampaign.critique2: Low demand, high price: Demand for green tariff is low, so economies of scale don't apply and unit costs remain high.
greenTariffCampaign.critique3: Lack of incentives: Instead of promoting renewable energy, the government positions green tariff as a luxury consumption.
greenTariffCampaign.critique4: Transparency issue: There is no strong independent audit mechanism to ensure your green premium actually goes to new renewable energy investments.
greenTariffCampaign.solution1Desc: If you have a detached house, install solar panels to generate your own electricity and sell the surplus to the grid.
greenTariffCampaign.solution2Desc: File complaints to EPDK and MPs for fair pricing of the green tariff.
greenTariffCampaign.solution3Desc: Where there is collective demand, prices drop. Share this campaign to spread green tariff awareness.
greenTariffCampaign.solution4Desc: Reducing consumption is more effective than switching to green tariff. Use A+ rated appliances, cut unnecessary usage.

whyEarth.title: 🌍 Why Must We Protect Earth?
whyEarth.subtitle: It's the only habitable place we know in the universe. We have no other chance.
whyEarth.footer: 🌱 Earth is our only home. Protecting it is not a choice, it's a necessity.

sections.title: 🪐 We Have No Other Home
sections.body: Despite all space exploration to date, no planet other than Earth has been found suitable for life. Examinations of celestial bodies like Mars, Venus, and the Moon show they are not suitable for human habitation. While the idea of establishing a colony on Mars looks great in science fiction films, in reality there is no breathable air, drinkable water, or atmosphere to protect us from the Sun's harmful radiation. Earth is a unique ecosystem built on a perfect balance with millions of species living on it. When we lose this balance, there is nothing we can replace it with.

sections.title: 🚀 Space Travel: Nothing More Than a Dream
sections.body: Traveling through space and establishing life on another planet is not possible with today's technology. The nearest star system, Proxima Centauri, is 4.24 light-years away. Even if we traveled at the speed of light, it would take us 4.24 years to get there. But with today's fastest spacecraft, this journey would take tens of thousands of years. Moreover, the problems to be encountered during this journey are endless:
items: Cosmic radiation: Without an atmosphere and magnetic field in space, astronauts are exposed to deadly levels of radiation.
Closed life cycle: Producing enough oxygen, water, and food during a multi-year journey is nearly impossible.
Effects of microgravity: Prolonged weightlessness causes muscle atrophy, bone density loss, and vision impairment.
Psychological factors: Being trapped in a small capsule for years has devastating effects on the human psyche.

sections.title: 🌱 What Must We Do for a Livable Earth?
sections.body: Keeping Earth habitable is essentially securing our own future. By reducing our carbon footprint, using natural resources consciously, recycling our waste, and switching to renewable energy, we can protect this unique planet. Remember: Earth is our home and we have no other. Taking care of it is our greatest responsibility to future generations.
